fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Tennessee Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity tennessee, how to transfer dietitian license to tennessee, dietitian compact tennessee, tennessee dietitian endorsement application

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by birth month) — no CE required for state renewal

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian/Nutritionist (LDN)
license_types.description: The standard credential required to practice dietetics or nutrition in Tennessee, regulated by the Board of Dietitian/Nutritionist Examiners under the Department of Health.

license_types.title: Temporary Permit
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have been approved by CDR to sit for the registration exam but have not yet passed it. Costs $5 and is valid until exam results are received. Requires a complete application on file.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program in dietetics, human nutrition, or a related field. Complete at least 1,200 hours of ACEND-accredited supervised practice.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. The CDR exam fee is $200.

steps.title: Submit the Online Application
steps.description: Apply through the Tennessee Online Application system. Pay the non-refundable $140 application fee. Include a passport-style photo and declaration of citizenship form.

steps.title: Complete Background Check & Provide Documentation
steps.description: Authorize and complete a criminal background check. Submit official transcripts, proof of CDR registration, verification of supervised practice completion, and a letter of recommendation.

documents.detail: Sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.
Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.
Documentation verifying completion of ACEND-accredited supervised practice hours.
Recent photo meeting standard passport specifications.
Mandatory background screening as part of the application process.

provisional_text: Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 to applicants who have completed all requirements except passing the CDR exam. The permit holder must have CDR authorization to test and a complete application on file with the Board. The permit is valid until exam results are received and does not require supervision.
reciprocity_text: Tennessee does not offer formal licensure by endorsement or reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Tennessee license through the standard application process, which requires current CDR registration, a complete application, and a background check. Holding an active CDR credential satisfies the education and examination requirements.

faqs.question: How much does a Tennessee dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application fee is $140, which includes the state regulatory fee. The CDR exam costs an additional $200. The biennial renewal fee is $90, due by the last day of your birth month.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee require continuing education for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Tennessee does not mandate state-level continuing education for license renewal. However, to maintain your CDR registration — which is required for state licensure — you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years.

faqs.question: Can I practice in Tennessee while waiting for my license?
faqs.answer: Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 for applicants who have CDR authorization to test. This permit allows practice while you await your exam results, provided your application is complete and on file with the Board.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: Tennessee has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Tennessee does not offer formal licensure by endorsement or reciprocity. Out-of-state dietitians must apply for a new Tennessee license through the standard application process, which requires current CDR registration, a complete application, and a background check. Holding an active CDR credential satisfies the education and examination requirements.
